title: Kick-off Happy Hour
description: We welcome participants to begin the Conference week at the Kick Off Happy Hour. This event is open to registered participants with a 2026 Conference Badge. Please note that Conference check-in closes at 5:30 PM on Monday, March 9.Light hors d’oeuvres and a cash bar will be available.

title: Quiet Space
Keynote Address Program
description: Join us on Tuesday, March 10th, at 9:00 AM in the Platinum 6 Ballroom to kick off the 41st Annual CSUN Assistive Technology Conference with a perceptive and impactful keynote address and awards presentation. Join us immediately after the Keynote Program for our Kaffeeklatsch with the Keynote at 11:00 AM in the Platinum 5 Ballroom, where you can engage directly with our Keynote Speaker and fellow Conference Participants. We look forward to gathering together and setting the stage for an engaging week!

title: Kaffeeklatsch with the Keynote
description: Join us for a relaxed, hour-long Kaffeeklatsch with the Keynote. This informal social hour offers attendees a unique opportunity to engage directly with Keynote Speaker Haley Moss and connect with fellow conference participants in a welcoming, conversational setting. Continue the dialogue sparked by the keynote, share ideas, ask questions, and build community, all while enjoying freshly served coffee. All are welcome to drop in, connect, and be part of the conversation.

title: A Braille STEM Game for Blind and Visually Impaired Children
description: Discover how refreshable Braille displays, haptic feedback, and gamification can transform STEM learning for blind and visually impaired children. Attendees will gain insights into inclusive design strategies and explore practical approaches for fostering engagement and equity in education.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how refreshable Braille displays and haptic feedback can scaffold STEM concepts.
Explore gamification strategies that sustain engagement and foster equity in STEM learning.

title: A Multimodal AI-Powered Travel Assistant Geo-App for BLV Users

presenters.name: Zhigang Zhu
presenters.affiliation: The City College of The City University of New York

presenters.name: Tyler Ortiz
presenters.affiliation: The City College of New York

description: This session details the development of Buddy Walk, an AI travel assistant mobile application designed to support independent travel for people with blindness and low vision (BLV). The process of designing, developing, and testing the application will be covered.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Accessibility Evolution: Past, Present, and Future Lessons
description: Through storytelling, reflection, and actionable takeaways, this session offers a roadmap for building a more inclusive future - one where accessibility is not just a regulation to comply with but a lived value that transforms opportunities.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Evaluate how post-ADA Title II regulations shift institutional responsibilities, and assess strategies advocates can use to hold organizations accountable for more inclusive practices.

Apply foresight frameworks to anticipate future trends in accessibility, and determine practical steps advocates can take to accelerate or redirect those trends to better align with the lived experience of disabled people.

title: AI for You: Everyday Strategies to Lighten Your Cognitive Load
description: Discover how AI can act as your thought partner to reduce overload and decision fatigue. Explore practical routines across planning, processing, decision support, communication, and reflection. Leave with prompts and a framework to protect focus, clarity, and well-being.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply the Offload → Simplify → Ground framework to reframe everyday tasks into repeatable AI routines that ease mental effort and generate clear, accessible outputs.
Draft and evaluate personalized AI prompts that use privacy guardrails, add verification checks, and produce accessible results in plain language with clear structure.

title: Amazon Entertainment Takes It to 11

presenters.name: Peter Korn
presenters.role: Director of Accessibility - Amazon Devices and Services

description: Explore Amazon's latest innovations in entertainment accessibility, including Dialogue Boost and advanced hearing aid connectivity on Fire TV. Learn how these features work together to create a more inclusive streaming experience.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn about Fire TV's latest hearing aid connectivity features and how they enhance the entertainment experience.
Discover how integrated accessibility features create a more inclusive streaming experience for viewers with disabilities.

title: Building Accessible iOS Apps with SwiftUI
description: Are you seeking practical skills to develop accessible SwiftUI apps? Come learn how to support VoiceOver, Switch Control, Large Text, Reduce Motion, and more. Discover the tools that make apps accessible to millions of users worldwide.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how to implement support for Large Text and Reduce Motion settings.
Gain familiarity with the Xcode Accessibility Inspector for debugging, and discover how to add custom accessibility actions to your app.

title: CANCELLED: Hard Truths,Brave Conversations: Race, Disability & Accessibility
description: Explore how race and disability intersect in accessibility work. Gain insights into systemic barriers, inclusive leadership, and strategies to elevate BIPOC voices across digital, workplace, and community spaces.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify systemic barriers BIPOC individuals face in disability communities.
Explore ways to amplify BIPOC voices in accessibility initiatives.

title: Digital Accessibility Trends and Insights for 2026
description: What has happened in the last year in accessibility and where is the industry likely to go in 2026. How can you position yourself to benefit to the maximum extent?
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: N/a
N/a

title: Digital Accessibility: Legal Update and Hot Topics
description: This session will explore the recent digital accessibility legal landscape and use recent observations and trends to propose best practices for organizational accessibility strategy, addressing user experience for people with disabilities, compliance requirements and risk mitigation.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Familiarity with recent trends in website accessibility litigation and investigations
Best practices for digital accessibility conformance and risk mitigation

title: Embedding Accessibility into AI Based Software Development
description: As AI transforms the way software is built—accelerating development cycles and enabling rapid prototyping—how do we ensure accessibility keeps pace? Join this session to explore how accessibility can be embedded into the new AI-powered workflows that define modern software development.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: With rapid prototyping as part of the new Software Development Life Cycle, learn when should accessibility be introduced—during ideation, before prototyping, before public preview or before GA.
Learn a roadmap for embedding accessibility into the DNA of fast-paced, AI-assisted development—ensuring that innovation doesn’t leave inclusion behind

title: Fine Tuning Slack's Screen Reader UX
description: Slack is collaboration software used for work and community projects. Come discover how Slack partners with blind people and experience a demo of the benefits yielded as a result. Questions, suggestions and feedback are welcome and encouraged.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: We'll showcase everything from how we've made it easier to navigate and reason about the state of the application, the work we've done to make conversations in channels and direct messages feel more natural and the settings we've built to give blind people the ability to customize their experience.
The ways anyone can provide feedback to Slack's Accessibility Team, as well as where to find our documentation for using Slack with a screen reader.

title: Improving Sign Language Recognition Accuracy with Multi-Cameras

presenters.name: Raja Kushalnagar
presenters.role: Director, Artificial Intelligence, Accessibility and Sign Languages Center (AIASL)

description: This session presents a multi-camera approach to improve ASL recognition by capturing signs from multiple angles, reducing occlusion, and enhancing accuracy of handshapes and non-manual markers. It highlights technical methods, ethical considerations, and applications for advancing accessible commun
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how multi-camera capture improves feature extraction of handshapes, movements, and non-manual markers.
Consider ethical and accessibility issues in designing and deploying multi-camera ASL recognition systems.

title: Lessons Learned Implementing Digital Accessibility in the Colorado Governor's Office
description: Operationalizing digital accessibility in a large, multi-branch office posed many challenges. Join us for key takeaways, pitfalls, and victories from our three-year journey tackling digital accessibility in the Colorado Governor's Office.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Digital accessibility is an evolving landscape that makes it nearly impossible to get everything exactly right. Strive for continual improvement — progress over perfection!

People are the most valuable resource! It’s important to have support networks in place so folks aren’t working in silos. The best investments we made were hiring a staff member to focus on document remediation and paying for manual web testing with members of the disability community.

title: Native Mobile App Accessibility:  Lessons Learned from myColorado

presenters.name: Robin Eckelberry
presenters.role: IT Software Engineer and Trainer - Accessibility

description: Native mobile apps pose accessibility challenges. Come learn from the Nelnet accessibility team’s experience evaluating a state-wide mobile app. Gain practical ideas you can use today!
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Leverage critical mobile testing tools.
Implement best practices for mobile accessibility testing, and utilize workarounds to address common mobile remediation challenges.

title: Remote Support Services:  A Gateway to Independent Living
description: Discover how Ohio’s Technology First Initiative and remote support model are transforming disability services. Learn practical strategies for scaling assistive technology, building partnerships, while improving independence, safety, and quality of life for individuals with IDD.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Expanding person-centered innovation through technology.
Learn strategies for scaling innovative service models across counties, regions and states.

title: Social-Haptic Communication for DeafBlind AT Instruction

presenters.name: Bryan Ward
presenters.affiliation: Helen Keller National Center for DeafBlind Youths and Adults
presenters.role: Is AI Good for the DeafBlind Community?

presenters.name: Tara Brown-Ogilvie
presenters.affiliation: Helen Keller National Center for DeafBlind Youths and Adults

description: This session will explore how social-haptic communication and other tactile strategies can enhance assistive technology instruction for DeafBlind learners. Drawing from HKNC research, presenters will share evidence-informed practices, resources, and implementation guidelines.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: 3.Provide resources that attendees can use to incorporate social-haptic signals into their own instructional practice. _x000D__x000D_4.Discuss guidelines for implementing social-haptic communication.
Disability Specific Topic Areas: Blind/Low Vision, Deaf/Hard of Hearing, and DeafBlind.

title: The Evolution of Accessibility: Compliance to Optimized Usability
description: Moving past checklists! Learn how Salesforce is shifting from compliance to creating optimized, inclusive user patterns. Discover how to balance legal requirements with cutting-edge usability for products that truly work for everyone.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: You'll learn how to move beyond static, rigid designs and create flexible, intuitive user patterns. Discover how designing for optimized usability can improve the user experience for everyone, ensuring your products are more than just compliant—they're truly inclusive and efficient.

You'll learn how to champion accessibility as a core business value, not just a box to check. Discover how shifting to a proactive, inclusive-by-design approach can become a strategic advantage that drives innovation and provides a superior user experience.

title: A New Era of Braille Access: Windows without Barriers
description: Redefining the Braille note taker for today. Powered by KeySoft on Microsoft Windows, this device delivers a braille-first, intuitive experience, making Windows navigation, learning, and productivity easier than ever.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will encounter how such users will gain transferable skills from the use of a note-taker to transition into a full screen-reader compatible Windows environment.
Understand how this device fosters learning Braille, and Windows screen reader commands,equipping users for success.

title: Accessibility Fairness in AI: Case Study on ChatGPT and Gemini
description: Generative AI tools like ChatGPT and Gemini are reshaping work, life and communication but people with disabilities (PWD) often face barriers. This session examines accessibility fairness—the extent to which PWD can access and benefit from generative AI tools to the same level as non-disabled users.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Accessibility in Action: Practical Insights and Proven Approaches
description: A fireside chat with Corbb O’Connor on the day-to-day realities of advancing digital accessibility. Learn to prioritize initiatives, avoid pitfalls & align teams. Gain strategies from real-world lessons in customer engagement, product development, & organizational strategy you can apply immediately.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn practical approaches for building accessibility into processes and cross-team collaboration.
Take away actionable ideas for improving customer engagement, product workflows, and reporting and Gain clarity on next steps they can apply within their own teams and organizations.

title: Advancing Inclusive Procurement with the EdTech Accessibility Dir

presenters.name: Christopher Phillips
presenters.affiliation: National Center on Accessible Digital Educational Materials & Instruction

presenters.name: Jena Fahlbush
presenters.affiliation: National Center on Accessible Digital Educational Materials & Instruction

description: Learn how the EdTech Accessibility Directory provides easy-to-use resources that support informed procurement decisions and effective vendor communication to ensure accessible digital learning experiences for students with disabilities.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify specific ways the directory can support informed decision-making processes when evaluating the accessibility of products during the procurement process.
Utilize and explain key technical features of the directory including the ability to search and contribute to product and vendor information.

title: AI PDF Conversion: Escaping Static Files into Semantic Freedom

presenters.name: Blake Bertuccelli-Booth
presenters.role: Assistant Director of Digital Accessibility Engineering

description: Learn how AI and MDX transform inaccessible PDFs into dynamic, open-source accessibility assets. Explore semantic markup, AI decision-making, and client-side transformations. Discover why open protocols, usable by people and language models, are essential for accessibility as a public good.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: How AI Tech Converts PDFs Into Accessibile Content: AI agents restructure raw content, MDX provides a universal semantic layer, and client-side tools turn content into interactive, multi-modal experiences. Together, these layers replace static PDFs with dynamic accessibility assets.

Why Open Source Matters: Accessibility must remain open. Open-source development avoids lock-in, blocks patents, and ensures shared semantic protocols that both people and AI can use. This creates consistent, sustainable accessibility across institutions.

title: CANCELED - Stump the Screen Reader
description: Step into our accessibility game show where the real challenge is bad UX! Audience members bring their most baffling components and apps, and our screen reader sleuth unpacks them live—revealing accessibility wins and fails.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: See how individual design and coding choices impact a screen reader user's ability to navigate and operate web content.
Have fun unpacking truly awful components.

title: Comparing Accessibility of Tooltips, Modals, Popups, Accordions
description: Compare the accessibility of tooltips, modals, popups, and accordions. Learn pitfalls, best practices, and strategies to design, test, and specify these components so all users can interact effectively.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify common pitfalls in implementation and how subtle differences in ARIA, focus, and markup can impact accessibility.
Learn practical design and testing strategies to ensure interactive patterns are accessible, usable, and inclusive.

title: Federal Agencies Promote Digital Accessibility: Industry Outreach

presenters.name: Betsy Sirk
presenters.role: Director, Digital Accessibility and Strategic Sourcing

description: The US Federal Government buys vastly more IT from Industry than it builds in-house. It relies on Industry to assess and report on the accessibility of its products. This session highlights the government-wide collaboration to engage Industry with clear, consistent messaging on what it needs.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: •Raising awareness of Federal Government digital accessibility laws and initiatives
•Identifying and addressing common digital accessibility challenges

title: From Boring to Playful: A Toy Solution for Braille Practice
description: Braille learning can feel repetitive and discouraging for children. This session showcases Slidot, a toy-like solution that makes practice playful and affordable. Discover how it boosted children’s motivation and braille skills, and explore how it can be adopted worldwide.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how Slidot offers a toy-like, low-cost solution that tunrs repetitive braille practice into palyful learning, providing immediate feedback and engaging interaction.

Explore pilot outcomes with children, including higher motivation, improved tactile recognition, and steadier study habits, confirmed by usage data and positive feedback, with potential for global adoption.

title: Inclusio: A One-Stop Shop for Interoperable, Accessible Graphics
description: Creating accessible graphics is difficult. Inclusio, a multi-team project, is exploring and developing ways to make it easy to generate and distribute interoperable, inclusive graphics with our end-to-end, AI-driven marketplace, open metadata format, and multimodal output systems.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply this information to search for, modify, convert, and create accessible graphics using this format
Generate interoperable content using Inclusio’s generation and authoring capabilities

title: Interactive Leadership Lab: Unlocking Accessibility Strategies
description: Engage in an interactive workshop tackling real accessibility challenges. Apply the drives of motivation to craft solutions, collaborate across product and design roles, and explore proven strategies from leading organizations. Leave with tools to embed accessibility practices in your organization.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will learn how Acquire, Bond, Challenge, and Defend can guide leadership behaviors to engage teams and embed accessibility into product and design workflows. They’ll leave with a framework for motivating designers, product managers, and engineers to take ownership of accessibility outcomes

Through case studies from Amazon, Cisco attendees will see how successful accessibility strategies are implemented and scaled. Actionable insights can be adapted to their own organizational context. The goal is to ensure accessibility becomes a core part of their organization’s culture and processes

title: Negotiating Accessibility: How to Turn Pushback into Progress

presenters.name: Radostina Tsvetkova
presenters.affiliation: Norwegian Directorate of Labour and Welfare (NAV)

description: Learn the negotiation strategies to navigate pushbacks with confidence and turn resistance into progress. Because accessibility shouldn’t be a battle. We can negotiate for progress, not compromise. When we’re told “no,” it’s not the end of the conversation, it’s where the negotiation begins.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore how key negotiation techniques  such as tactical empathy, labeling, mirroring, calibrated questions, and no-oriented questions can strengthen communication, improve collaboration, and translate directly into accessibility advocacy.
Gain hands-on experience through real-world accessibility scenarios that show how these techniques turn pushback into progress, and that hearing “no” marks the beginning of negotiation, not the end.

title: New Kindle Features Advancing Inclusive Reading
description: Explore groundbreaking accessibility features transforming Kindle, from enhanced UI font sizing to improved navigation for screen readers. Discover how updates in text-to-speech, reading comprehension tools, and our shopping and publishing experiences create a more inclusive reading experience.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Describe [3+] new Kindle features in depth and understand where and how to access them.
Learn how Kindle’s commitment to creating accessible experiences results in a better experience for all.

title: Practical Approaches to Prevent Litigation and Regulatory Review

presenters.name: Matthew Luken
presenters.role: SVP, Global Programs & Regulatory Affairs; European Partnerships

description: It is possible (and practical) to take a proactive approach to litigation and regulatory review. Learn of several approaches, how to enact internal conversations, and how to speak the language of your colleagues.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how to create a conversation that causes your colleagues to lean in and ask you to tell them more.
Learn how to shift from a reactive approach to a proactive approach for your program.

title: Tech4Me: Plug in and Power up Competitive Integrated Employment

presenters.name: Jordan Wagner
presenters.affiliation: The Ohio State University Nisonger Center

presenters.name: Jessie Green
presenters.affiliation: The Ohio State University Nisonger Center

presenters.name: Andrew Buck
presenters.affiliation: The Ohio State University Nisonger Center
presenters.role: Research Assistant Professor and LEND Special Education Faculty Member

description: People with developmental disabilities face unique challenges to employment success. In today’s rapidly evolving workplace, technology can be a powerful equalizer. To help bridge the gap between tech & workplace outcomes, Tech4Me features a toolkit with strategies to evaluate needs & leverage tech.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand the Tech4Me approach to evaluating and prioritizing need areas and how that may lead to identification of technology solutions that are relevant to specific individuals.

Identify at least 8 examples of technology solutions and how each may be leveraged to reduce barriers to (1) transportation and navigation to and around work, (2)  preparing for work, (3) getting work done, and (4) interacting with others.

title: Usability and Accessibility Regression: A Live-User Data Study
description: Discover data-driven insights from hundreds of live-user audits showing why accessibility gaps are increasing despite automation and awareness. Learn which usability barriers are most often missed and gain practical strategies to create more inclusive digital experiences.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify the most common accessibility barriers uncovered through live-user testing, including issues often missed by automated tools and how to effectively build strategies to implement fixes for these barriers.

Apply data-driven strategies to improve the accessibility and usability of digital products by integrating live-user testing, leveraging automation appropriately, and prioritizing remediation workflows that address real-world barriers impacting people with disabilities.

title: Vispero & Everway: Enterprise Solutions for Inclusive Workplaces
description: Vispero and Everway share enterprise best practices for managing assistive technology in the workplace. Learn strategies for deployment, support, and inclusion to ensure employees have the tools they need to succeed.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore strategies to streamline IT support and ensure compatibility with workplace systems.
Understand how proactive planning fosters long-term inclusion and employee success.

title: A Sound Education: Demystifying Audio Description in Higher Ed

presenters.name: Brian McNeilly
presenters.affiliation: University of California, Office of the President

description: Have videos that need to be made accessible? Heard of captions, but aren't sure if your video needs audio description? This session has you covered! We'll focus on what audio description is, how you can include it in your content, and how to share that newly accessible content far & wide.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will know how to author audio description tracks for a variety of content common in higher education settings.
Attendees will be able to articulate a variety of methods to upload a video with audio descriptions.

title: Accessibility at Scale: Maximizing Coverage and Quality
description: Digital accessibility remains a significant challenge as digital ecosystems grow, and traditional methods cannot keep up with today’s scale. This session explores how combining automation, expert review, and continuous monitoring improves coverage and helps teams scale accessibility effectively.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: How a hybrid model that blends automation, expert review, and continuous monitoring overcomes the barriers that prevent organizations from making real progress.
A candid discussion about where AI meaningfully supports accessibility at scale and where human expertise remains essential.

title: Accessible Science Experiments for Visually Impaired Students

presenters.name: Piyush Chanana
presenters.affiliation: National Centre for Assistive Health  Technologies, Indian Institute of  Technology Delhi

description: Learn how tactile kits, auditory cues, and inclusive lab models make science experiments accessible for students with visual impairments, enabling independent learning and advancing inclusive education practices.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: ADA Title II and You: Document Accessibility in Your Office
description: With ADA deadlines nearing, many organizations face challenges of document accessibility. Presenters will share WCAG regulations with tips for various document formats, highlight benefits for users with disabilities, and provide resources to foster accessibility and advocacy.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: 2.Relevant anecdotes from state and local agencies on their compliance goals
3.Actionable recommendations to make documents of all types more accessible, including suggestions for training, free resources, and community advocacy

title: AI for Accessibility: Real-World Innovations Empowering All
description: Discover real-world examples of AI driving accessibility, from sign language translation to voice banking, navigation, and assistive robotics. Learn how these innovations empower independence, improve communication, and shape inclusive futures across education, healthcare, and daily life.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will learn about processes within Intuit used to improve employees knowledge and perception of AI.

title: AI, Wearable and Community Driven Innovation
description: Discover how AI & wearables are revolutionizing independence for the blind/low-vision community! Join us to explore AI progress, demo EchoVision smart glasses, and hear user stories proving tech’s power when co-created with users.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: See the importance of co-creating with blind and low-vision users to ensure usability and trust.
Understand the functions provided by the cutting edge AI wearable

title: Evo-Web: An A11Y-First Approach to Building UI Components at eBay
description: Evo-Web brings eBay’s Evo Design Playbook to life as reusable UI components for the web. Learn how engineers shape accessibility early by reviewing patterns, anticipating interaction issues, and ensuring WCAG-aligned, inclusive components from the start.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply strategies for collaborating with designers to identify and resolve accessibility gaps early.
Use Evo-Web and the Evo Design Playbook as examples of how design systems and engineering libraries can work together to ensure WCAG compliance.

title: From Pre-Braille to Data Fluency: Developing Tactile Skills
description: Explore how dynamic multiline braille displays like the Dot Pad X are redefining tactile literacy. Learn from current research and pilots how students develop from early tactile skills to fluently interpreting and creating complex data—and what this means for the future of inclusion.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify strategies and tools that support this progression using dynamic multiline braille displays.
Articulate the expanded learning outcomes possible when blind students are given access to spatial and data-rich content in real time.

title: Is AI Good for the DeafBlind Community?

presenters.name: Bryan Ward
presenters.affiliation: Helen Keller National Center for DeafBlind Youths and Adults
presenters.role: Is AI Good for the DeafBlind Community?

description: Artificial intelligence is becoming deeply integrated into daily life, but is it accessible and beneficial for people who are DeafBlind? This interactive session will address that question through a direct conversation with professionals who support the DeafBlind community every day.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore current trends in AI accessibility relevant to the DeafBlind community
Increase awareness of real-world applications and challenges of AI from the perspectives of professionals working with DeafBlind individuals

title: Smartphone Training to Enhance Life Skills of Thai Blind
description: Learn how a TAB smartphone training program equips Thai blind individuals with essential life skills and digital confidence. The session presents curriculum design, teaching strategies, and outcome evidence to inspire inclusive technology initiatives across cultures.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Examine instructional methods that foster self-directed technology use.
Apply a scalable, culturally responsive framework for inclusive digital education.

title: Strengthening AT Consideration with the AT Decision-Making Tool

presenters.name: Heather Bridgman
presenters.role: Assistive Technology and Accessible Educational Materials Specialist

description: Learn how the Assistive Technology Decision-Making Tool (ATDMT) supports IEP teams in considering the need for AT and accessible educational materials (AEM). The ATDMT is freely available for use by teams across the country and beyond. Implementation strategies will be shared.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify 2 resources available to facilitate the AT and AEM assessment process.
List 2 strategies that teams could use to facilitate AT Consideration using the ATDMT in other states or regions.

title: Supporting Students with Blindness in Denmark
description: The presentation outlines Denmark’s vision support system and the roles ensuring tailored help for students with blindness. It also shows how audio support strengthens braille learning, independence, and motivation through innovative teaching methods.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: How to use Braille with audio feedback for beginners using a Braille display
How Braille devices are used in mainstream education and in special education

title: The Role of Typing Instruction in School Education and Dyslexia

presenters.name: Miwa Murata
presenters.affiliation: Takasaki University of Health and Walfare

description: We will discuss the typing skills required for computers in this new era, how typing skills are handled in school education, and the approach to teaching typing to students with learning disabilities.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: The acquisition of typing skills among Japanese elementary and junior high school students, for which data is currently unavailable, will be clarified.
Data for discussing the positioning of assessments for kanji and English writing in Japanese school education.

title: Write It Right: Crafting Acceptance Criteria for Success
description: Use accessibility acceptance criteria to set your teams on a path for success! This session provides a framework for writing concrete, well-crafted acceptance criteria that build accessibility into every step from the start. Avoid the pitfalls and leave with skills that foster shared understanding.
