presenters.affiliation: Wood County Board of Developmental Disabilities

description: This session will provide a hands-on overview of a variety of AT solutions and community partnerships to support independent living, develop age-friendly communities, and promote aging in place.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Develop 1 action step to integrate 1 strategy presented in the learner’s local community to contribute to the development of an age-friendly society.
Identify 3 resources available for obtaining AT for the aging population.

title: Building Inclusive Mindsets: A Scalable Research Framework

presenters.name: Lauren Britton
presenters.role: Lead UX Researcher and Accessibility Strategist

description: Learn about the Mindsets: a scalable, inclusive alternative to personas. This framework focuses on the purpose of human engagement with your company, creating a universal foundation that ensures scalability while saving time and money.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Tips for researching and building Mindsets
How to scale and implement the framework for their organization

title: College Transition Resources for Visually Impaired Students

presenters.name: Mina Lamarra
presenters.role: Student (class of 2026), Huntington Beach High School

presenters.name: Stacy Branham
presenters.role: Associate Professor, Department of Informatics, UC Irvine

presenters.name: Khanh Tran
presenters.role: Student (class of 2026), Bolsa Grande High School

description: Hear directly from visually impaired high school students about their experiences seeking resources to support their transition into college. Attendees will learn what resources exist, how to find them, and strategies for advocacy and allyship.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn effective strategies as visually impaired students to identify resources to prepare for college.
Prepare as family members, teachers, and allies to support college-bound visually impaired students.

title: Enhancing Rules-Based Automated Testing with Machine Learning Inference
description: Learn how machine learning–based inference can extend accessibility testing beyond rules-based checks. This session explains how ML works in this context, what it can reveal that DOM inspection misses, and demonstrates examples of how these methods can be applied in practice.
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how machine learning and AI can extend accessibility test coverage.
See practical examples of ML-based inference in automated testing, and explore how real-time remediation can support end users with disabilities.

title: Feature vs. Foundation: Google on AI & Accessibility

presenters.name: Daniel Tarekegn
presenters.role: Senior Program Manager Lead, Search Accessibility

description: Should accessible AI be an assistive tech feature (like in TalkBack) or a foundation (like in Gemini)? Explore this strategic tension with Google, examining trade-offs across Search’s AI Mode, Project Astra’s Visual Interpreter, and more. Leave ready to advocate for an AI future truly built for all.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Analyze the trade-offs and ethical considerations of each approach, using real-world examples from Google (e.g., TalkBack, Gemini, Project Astra).
Evaluate which strategy (feature vs. foundation) is more suitable for solving different types of accessibility challenges.

title: How to Influence Change as an Accessibility Advocate
description: This session equips accessibility advocates with practical strategies to influence change from any role. Attendees will learn how to build allies across functions, reframe resistance into opportunity, and embed accessibility into projects, workflows, and team culture.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn methods to gain credibility, build allies, reframe resistance, and influence others to create change as an accessibility advocate.
Gain knowledge of practical approaches to embed accessibility into your projects, workflows, team culture, or company.

title: Navigation Robot in Exhibition Spaces for People with Vision Loss
description: Discover the "AI Suitcase," a navigation robot for people with visual impairment. This session shares key findings from a exhibition space trial, including user feedback and performance data. Learn how this assistive technology can enhance accessibility in museums and other public venues.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: High affinity between the navigation robot for visually impaired people and exhibition spaces
Further requests, opinions, and discussions from people with visual impairments regarding the robot's functions and the facilities where it can be used

title: Promoting Connection and Communication with AAC and AT
description: Discover how to support early communication in children with complex needs using AAC and assistive technology. This session covers practical strategies to empower families and professionals across disciplines, offering tools that promote connection and access.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Describe the importance of early access to communication for non-speaking and disabled children.
Develop strategies to empower families, educators and caregivers in fostering communication across everyday routines.

title: Route Selection by GPS Reliability for the Visually Impaired
description: Navigating with GPS can be unreliable in dense urban areas, posing serious risks for visually impaired (VI) individuals who rely on precise positioning for safety and independence. This session presents a machine learning–based system that predicts GPS reliability by analyzing features such as building height, density, and satellite visibility.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: The A11y of Driving
description: With automotive interfaces becoming like apps on wheels, how do accessibility and design principles fit in? Can accessibility and automation open up safe driving for seniors and people with certain disabilities?
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand the unique challenges and requirements that automotive interfaces pose to accessibility and UX design.
Increased levels of automation will create new scenarios that can extend driving to people who are older or have certain disabilities.

title: W3C APA Note Accessibility Maturity Model Update
description: The APA W3C Accessibility Maturity Model Task Force will provide an overview of their Maturity Model publication and its intended use. They’ll share plans for future work and tooling that’s under development. You’re invited to review the publication at https://www.w3.org/TR/maturity-model/
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Benefits of using the W3C's Accessibility Maturity Model
Improvements planned for future work on the W3C's Accessibility Maturity Model

title: What We Wish We Knew: Five Years and Three Design Systems Later
description: Accessibility isn’t an afterthought, it’s the foundation. We’ll share how we built accessibility into the Dow Jones multi-brand design system, the missteps and lessons learned along the way, and how designers, engineers, and accessibility pros teamed up to keep it simple, usable, and effective.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Show how to approach interactions, accessibility, and design standards across brands within the same company
Review some of the hurdles we’ve experienced to show where to keep things complicated and where to simplify the design system as a whole

title: Karaoke
description: Karaoke returns for an energetic evening centered on music, performance, and shared fun.Light refreshments and a cash bar will be available.

title: Featured Presentation: Assistive Technology at Meta: Innovations with Ray-Ban Meta and Neural Band
description: Join us for an engaging conversation featuring leading members from Meta, each bringing unique expertise in the field of assistive technology. This session will spotlight Meta’s groundbreaking work with Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses and the Neural Band, exploring how recent research and innovations could transform accessibility and empower users.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: N/A

title: Quiet Space
A11Y Research: Tools & Techniques for Meaningful Interviews
description: Learn how to conduct accessibility interviews that uncover real barriers. Gain tools, example questions, and techniques for planning, probing, and sharing insights that complement usability testing and drive action.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Show how to analyze and share interview findings with clips, stories, and simple metrics that amplify participant voices and influence organizational change.

title: Accessibility Checklists Lie: What They Miss and Why It Matters
description: Go beyond checklists to uncover what automated tools miss. Learn a 7-step process and practical strategies to evaluate document accessibility, combining standards with real user feedback to create content that truly works for everyone.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Discover a 7-step checklist to catch accessibility issues that automated tools often miss.
Explore simple ways to include user feedback and testing in document workflows.

title: Aligning Organizational Priorities with Accessibility Metrics
description: Why measure Accessibility Progress? Which metrics matter most? Learn how to articulate progress and demonstrate the strength of your digital accessibility program to communicate your effort, increase your impact, influence stakeholder buy-in, and make the case for greater accessibility for all.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Track and analyze accessibility progress with key indicators of your digital accessibility program's strength:Use automated and manual assessment tools to monitor compliance, remediation, and training rates. Learn to identify trends and patterns in accessibility findings, and set goals.

Communicate impact and progress: Tailor reports for different audiences, demonstrate the ROI of accessibility efforts, and build a compelling case for ongoing investment. Discover strategies for sharing progress, celebrating wins, and positioning accessibility as an ongoing journey.

title: Beyond Alt Text: Building Interactive, User-Driven Infographics
description: Learn how to move beyond alt text by transforming static infographics into interactive, navigable PDFs. Discover tagging, landmarks, and structures that let users explore complex visuals independently with assistive technologies.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand effective use of nested tags and landmarks to divide content into logical parts.
Enable user-driven exploration of complex visuals beyond alt text.

title: Crossing the Cattle Guard: From Isolation to Inclusion

presenters.name: Jennifer Wenzel
presenters.affiliation: American Printing House for the blind

description: O&M, social skills, and assistive technology competence are crucial factors for long-term success. The Monarch offers new teaching tools for these subjects, especially for students in isolated areas. Follow one student’s journey to learn more.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate how the WingIt app aids learning in the Expanded Core.
Share how the Monarch’s Tactile Graphics Image Library fosters learning of visual concepts which aids students socially.

title: From ACRs to Action: AI Reveals Accessibility Gaps by Community
description: Discover how AI can transform Accessibility Conformance Reports by scoring them based on important communities your organization serves—enabling more efficient processes and smarter vendor choices.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how creating an AI bot can be a straightforward process and how the instructions, knowledge, and capabilities sections all contribute to the quality of the generated reports.

title: Inside the VPAT: A Conversation with the Authors
description: This discussion with VPAT experts and authors will give attendees an exclusive look into the updates in VPAT® Version 2.6 and the process behind its development. The session will also address frequently asked VPAT questions, providing practical insights for VPAT authors and reviewers.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: •Discuss some of the frequently asked VPAT questions
•Improve VPAT education for both authors and reviewers

title: Lived Experience as Critical Data: An Inclusive Research Method
description: Ready to prove the value of lived experience? This session offers a rigorous qualitative method to turn personal stories into actionable data. You'll learn to interview and analyze insights from multiple perspectives to inform policy and strategy, counter bias, and drive true inclusion.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Hone interviewing skills that prioritize the people who generously share their lived experience and expertise
Cultivate data analysis skills to retain the voice of the people and surface their purposes and priorities to co-design solutions

title: Making ArcGIS ESRI Maps Accessible

presenters.name: Brandon Biggs
presenters.affiliation: XR Navigation and Smith-Kettlewell Eye Research Institute

description: This session covers built-in tools, ADA compliance requirements, and real-world strategies, including case studies, for non-visual, color-blind, and keyboard accessibility for ArcGIS. Attendees will gain practical skills to meet regulations and serve all their users.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how to obtain full ADA compliance and non-visual accessibility with the Audiom widget.
Learn how to obtain full ADA compliance without the Audiom widget.

title: Non-visual Design: Tactile Graphics Built in SVG
description: We'll teach you how to create graphics through hand-coding SVG. You'll learn techniques and resources for authoring and interpreting the output, to give BLV people graphical literacy skills and tools useful in education, work, and personal life.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand available methods that blind designers can use for graphic review, including tactile production methods and visual interpretation.
Learn basic design considerations for SVG authors engaged in tactile graphic design.

title: Sign Language Interaction with Alexa: The Impact of Sticky Hands

presenters.name: Paige DeVries
presenters.role: Graduate Student, Accessible Human-Centered Computing and Policy

presenters.name: Raja Kushalnagar
presenters.role: Director, Artificial Intelligence, Accessibility and Sign Languages Center (AIASL)

description: This session discusses a first-of-its-kind study on hypothetical sign language interaction with Amazon Alexa in a kitchen environment. In this environment, users frequently get their hands dirty, which both limits touchscreen based interactions with Alexa and affects the way deaf and hard of hearing.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: The Zero Project’s Equitable AI Alliance: Bridging Innovation and Inclusion
description: The Zero Project is advancing Equitable AI to ensure persons with disabilities are not left behind. This session shares global insights, standards, and case studies on how AI can be developed and deployed inclusively.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify international standards and frameworks (e.g., EN 301 549, CRPD, JTC 21 CEN....) that can guide inclusive AI development and support accessibility compliance across sectors.
Explore practical case studies and collaborative models from the Zero Project network that show how equitable AI can be implemented globally.

title: Walled Gardens: Open Accessibility in a Fragmented Ecosystem
description: Explore how closed ecosystems obstruct accessibility and how open-source models offer a more inclusive path forward. We’ll critique current platform silos and propose actionable strategies for creating a more interoperable, testable, and inclusive tech landscape.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Contrast open-source vs proprietary approaches to accessibility.
Inspire attendees to advocate for cross-platform transparency and interoperability.

title: Accessible Assessments through Question Test Interoperability 3.0
description: Unlock the potential of Question and Test Interoperability (QTI) 3.0 to enhance accessible assessments. Learn how QTI 3.0 supports interoperability, leverages HTML5 accessibility features, and discover best practices for creating inclusive assessments. Perfect for educators and technologists.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Discover how to leverage HTML accessibility features in your assessments.
Gain insights into best practices for creating inclusive assessments.

title: AccessTrader: A Bridge to Financial Independence for All
description: What if trading were finally accessible? AccessTrader is an AI-powered assistant for blind, low-vision, and beginner users. With voice interaction, screen reader-friendly charts, and plain language, it unlocks financial inclusion and empowers users to participate in wealth-building on equal terms.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn about AccessTrader and its accessibility features, including voice interaction, screen reader-compatible charts, and plain language guidance
Learn how to use AccessTrader and gain financial knowledge

title: CANCELLED: Object Comprehension and Representation by the Congenitally Blind
description: This session presents the first large-scale, systematic study of 36 congenitally blind students who sketched both familiar solids and less familiar objects designed to test sensitivity to proportion and topology.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Creating Content for Neurodivergent Learners
description: Create content that is inclusive and understandable for neurodivergent learners. Learn about plain language, meaningful structure, and universal design for learning.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Create meaningful structure.
Employ universal design for learning.

title: DA2: The Next Generation NLS Digital Talking Book Player

presenters.name: Alysha Hiller
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

presenters.name: Zhi Huang
presenters.affiliation: National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled, Library of Congress

description: Unlock a world of reading with the new NLS digital talking book player, the DA2, available for free to NLS patrons. Explore its seamless features for all ages and tech comfort levels, and learn how it empowers individuals with print disabilities to expand and customize their reading experience.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how to interact with BARD directly and navigate BARD content on the DA2.
Understand how to customize the DA2 to meet individual needs.

title: From Compliance to Culture-Scaling Document Accessibility with AI
description: Learn how large financial institutions can move beyond ad-hoc compliance fixes to scalable, AI-powered accessibility. Discover strategies, real-world lessons, and practical tools to embed accessibility into workflows, reducing risk, cutting costs, and building inclusive practices.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Discuss how a large, well-known investment firm uses an enterprise-wide approach to embedding accessibility into disclosures, reports, and client communications.
Explore practical strategies and tools, automation, workflows, templates, and expert support for scaling accessibility efficiently.

title: How to embed AI into an Accessibility Workflow
description: AI is advancing by leaps and bounds, but the good news is it is an environment that you can tune to get what you want in your environment, including really accessible outcomes. In this session, we will show you some cool tricks to increase the likelihood of good outcomes for you and your team(s).
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: N/A
N/A

title: Inclusive AI: Tips for Developers, Tech Trainers, and Advocates
description: This presentation shares findings on how AI impacts people with disabilities, highlighting benefits, barriers, and user experiences. It offers recommendations for developers, AT trainers, and advocates to help ensure AI tools are accessible and meet diverse user needs.

title: Making Digital Accessibility Training Actually Work
description: Join me to think through professional development in accessibility in a holistic manner and leave with motivation and ideas about how to initiate and maintain a successful digital accessibility training effort.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Specify at least four considerations that help to determine training topics and priorities.
Identify and discuss at least five possible measures of a training program’s effectiveness.

title: Making Research Inclusive through Accessible Survey Design
description: Learn practical strategies to design accessible surveys and research workflows that support users of Assistive Technology and facilitate inclusive research. Examine common survey platforms and apply accessibility techniques in this interactive session.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Participants will be able to identify three common accessibility barriers in survey platforms along with strategies to overcome them to create more accessible survey instruments.
Participants will be able to demonstrate and apply appropriate accessibility techniques to create more accessible surveys that support diverse AT users.

title: Smart Wheelchair with Semi and Fully Autonomous Navigation

presenters.name: Christian Mandel
presenters.affiliation: German Research Center for Artificial Intelligence DFKI

description: This session presents smart wheelchair prototypes developed within the EU-funded REXASI-PRO project. We present the usage of advanced sensors, natural language interfaces, and a novel 3D Driving Assistant that prevents collisions by modifying unsafe commands. We demonstrate two use cases: driver-controlled operation and fully autonomous navigation.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Transforming Accessibility: AI Approaches for Higher Education
description: Student support leaders face rising caseloads and compliance pressures. This session shows how AI-enabled, data-driven systems help directors and program managers modernize operations, expand capacity, and deliver sustainable, inclusive support across campus.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Design cross-department workflows that unify student support functions (counseling, tutoring, housing, disability services).
Leverage secure, validated data to advocate for resources and build institutional credibility.

title: WCAG Without the Jargon: A Beginner’s Guide
description: Learn WCAG without the jargon. This beginner-friendly session explains guidelines in plain language, shows real-world examples, and provides a simple roadmap to help you start applying accessibility standards with confidence.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Recognize common digital barriers that WCAG is designed to address.
Connect the language of the guidelines to real-world user experiences.

title: Advancing Neurodiverse Inclusion with Conversational AI

presenters.name: Jen Hickey
presenters.affiliation: Applied Social Media Lab at Harvard University

presenters.name: Brendan Miller
presenters.affiliation: Applied Social Media Lab at Harvard University's Berkman Klein Center

presenters.name: Nicole Brennan
presenters.affiliation: Applied Social Media Lab at Harvard University

description: Discover how large language models can enhance inclusion for neurodiverse users. Learn about AI tools that support accessibility, engagement, and agency in online and in-person conversations. Join us to help shape inclusive tech!
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: The benefits and drawbacks of using LLMs to increase neurodiverse participation.
The chance to be a part of an open source experimentation and learning community exploring these ideas.

title: EmotiBit: Open-Source Biometric Tools for Assistive Innovation
description: EmotiBit is an open-source, research-grade biometric sensor that empowers accessible, DIY innovation in assistive technology. With open data and customizable workflows, EmotiBit empowers individuals and communities to design inclusive tools and advance accessibility.
secondary_topics: Virtual Reality (VR)/Augmented Reality (AR)/Cross and Mixed Reality (XR)
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn about what makes EmotiBit a great choice for the DIY community. EmotiBit is low cost, open source, and provides the user with 100% data ownership - lowering the barrier of entry for who can use it and what it can be used for.
Learn about the different applications that companies and university labs have used EmotiBit for

title: Fix Accessibility Processes Using the Web Accessibility Framework
description: Discover how the Web Accessibility Framework can help your organization achieve and maintain its accessibility goals. Learn practical strategies to set goals, fix processes, and conduct regular testing to ensure your websites remain accessible.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore the key components—Identify, Prevent, Detect, Respond, and Remediate.
Gain practical suggestions for implementing an accessibility framework in your organization to not only fix issues but also fix processes.

title: Generative AI’s Potential to Supporting Deficits in Reading Comprehension

presenters.name: Lisa Sousa
presenters.role: Generative AI’s Potential to Supporting Deficits in Reading Comprehension

description: This session explores LLMs as an emerging assistive technology for students with learning disabilities, particularly reading comprehension deficits. Attendees will be introduced to a decision making framework (COAT framework).
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will be able to describe methods for LLM use to increase outcomes for students with specific learning disabilities in reading comprehension.
Participants will provide strengths and limitations of GAI practice for SLD learners.

title: My WCAG Wishlist: Closing Gaps Between Rules and Reality
description: Meeting WCAG is just the start. Compliance without usability leaves disabled users behind. This session delivers a wishlist of improvements that challenge the status quo and encourage teams to develop digital products that consider all of their users.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Participants will be able to identify best practices that extend beyond compliance to improve user experience.
Participants will be able to apply a checklist of practical techniques within design, development, and QA processes to create more inclusive digital products.

title: Physical Activity Tools for People Who Are Blind or Low Vision
description: Experience hands-on strategies and innovative equipment that break down barriers to physical activity for people who are blind or low vision. Leave inspired with tools you can use across education, fitness, and recreation to promote true inclusion.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply strategies to implement inclusive physical activities across various educational, recreational, and community settings.
Explore inclusive design principles to create supportive environments that promote participation for individuals with visual impairments

title: Practices for Continuous Accessibility
description: Treating accessibility as a core concern leads to better user experience, reduced risk, and improved business outcomes. But how do you make it the default? Learn how to drive organizational change through routine, metrics, and technology that fits your processes.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Document and communicate accessibility wins to demonstrate value.
Drive organizational change and gain buy-in from cross-functional teams._x000D_Establish a repeatable routine for continuous accessibility improvements.

title: Smartwatch Usability and Accessibility Across Visual Abilities

presenters.name: Hyung Kim
presenters.affiliation: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University

description: This session presents research findings on smartwatch usability and accessibility for sighted users and users with visual disabilities. Attendees will learn evidence-based interaction challenges and design considerations that support inclusive use, with implications for improving user interfaces and accessibility in research and applied contexts.
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Testing ICT Hardware for Multi-Standard Compliance
description: Testing for ICT hardware like TV remotes, televisions, kiosks, exhibits, and EV charges takes a nuanced approach. Learn how Deque looks across published standards and other documentation in a consistent an scalable way to help ensure that hardware entering the market is meeting PwDs needs.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Examine how to make these processes repeatable and scalable.
Learn how to extrapolate this approach nearly any piece of hardware.

title: Using AI to Reframe Digital Communication

presenters.name: David Martin
presenters.role: Global Compliance & Partner Management Leader, Accessibility

description: Stop agonizing over unclear texts. This practical session will show beginners how to build your own AI tool to reframe ambiguous communications, reduce anxiety, and foster digital confidence. Enhance your assistive technology toolkit.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Witness a live demonstration of AI providing immediate, alternative perspectives on ambiguous communications.
Learn a practical, step-by-step technique to build your own tone-assessing AI tool using readily available platforms, to reduce anxiety and prevent negative interaction spirals.

title: What's New with Apple Accessibility
description: Learn how to use Apple’s new built-in accessibility features including Accessibility Reader, Magnifier for Mac, and Live Listen with Live Captions. Designed to support vision, hearing, speech, cognitive, and mobility, these features enable people in daily living, work, and creativity.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explain how to navigate and control iPad and iPhone with Eye Tracking and Head Tracking.
Learn how to use Magnifier for Mac for a seamless reading experience.

title: When "Satisfied" Isn't Satisfying: ACE-L, an Accessibility Scale
description: Research within accessibility populations consistently shows satisfaction (CSat) is inflated compared to non-accessibility groups. New metrics have been developed to provide a more accurate reflection of the user experience. Reinvent how you measure, track, and understand people's experience.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore the creation of a new measurement tool, walking through the creation of a scale that more closely aligns with the accessibility user experience to better reflect people's opinions and needs.

title: Birds of a Feather: Compliance in Practice
description: Whether you’re responsible for policy, procurement, digital accessibility, or organizational compliance, this Birds of a Feather session invites you to connect with others working to meet ADA Title II and EAA mandates. In an informal, discussion-driven setting, participants can share real-world experiences, raise questions, and collaborate on understanding complex requirements. Come build community and strengthen your compliance toolkit.Hosts: Kate Tipton and Joseph Krack

title: AI & AT: Navigating the Ethics of Bias and User Agency
description: Get the tools you need to confront the real ethical risks of AI. This session tackles algorithmic bias and user autonomy in AT, offering practical strategies and guidelines for deploying AI ethically and confidently in your own work.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Develop strategies to maintain user control and agency and ensure technology empowers rather than replaces human decision-making.
Apply a practical framework to analyze ethical dilemmas and create actionable guidelines for deploying AI responsibly in their own settings.

title: Building a Culture of Accessibility Leadership
description: Explore how leaders can embed accessibility into culture, strategy, and practice. Learn to influence teams, overcome resistance, and sustain inclusion beyond compliance.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Executives & Business Owners
Project Managers & Team Leads

title: Deafblind Inclusion by the Blind: A Case Study

presenters.name: Takahiro Miura
presenters.affiliation: National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST)

description: This study reports on the facilitation practices provided by visually impaired students to support a deafblind peer in higher education. We describe the communication modalities employed (tactile sign language, finger Braille, text interpretation), the iterative facilitation process through interviews and feedback.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Designing SONY’s TV Remote Together with Users
description: This session showcases Sony’s strong commitment to inclusive design through co-creation with end-users. By presenting our newly updated TV remote, we highlight how collaboration with over 50 participants helped us achieve a meaningful and user-centered outcome.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Highlight Sony’s strong commitment to inclusive design and accessibility across its televisions.
Share key insights and outcomes from collaborative sessions with over 50 participants that shaped the final product.

title: Empower Independence and Enhance Capacity with Remote Support
description: This session showcases the innovative partnership between SimplyHome and East Bay Innovations (EBI), demonstrating how their blended remote support model of 24/7/365 is transforming services for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Explore Provider Capacity Gains:_x000D_Discover how East Bay Innovation leverages technology to enhance staff capacity and reduce the reliance on in-person staffing while maintaining high-quality support.

Review Implementation Best Practices:_x000D_Gain insights into the collaborative process between a remote support provider and service agency, including assessments, planning, and adapting to diverse support needs.

title: Get the Most Out of GitHub with Continuous AI for Accessibility
description: Learn how to accelerate accessibility by integrating custom agents and automation throughout your team’s development process.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how to create custom agents and configure them to run automatically at the right moments as your team collaborates.
Learn how to optimize GitHub Copilot to improve accessibility.

title: Kurzweil: Math Reading That Works
description: Using Kurzweil to read math does not require you to eliminate other AT solutions that provide TTS for your students. Kurzweil can add an affordable solution to your campus for testing and day-to-day use for students without having to learn MathML.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Practical examples of how to weave Kurzweil math reading into your current workflow.
Experiment with a variety of math formats and learn how to get these formats to read math with minimal remediation.

title: LG Electronics Accessibility: Inclusive Design through Education

presenters.name: Jiyeon Lee
presenters.role: LG Electronics Accessibility: Inclusive Design through Education & Community

presenters.name: Sera Park
presenters.role: LG Electronics Accessibility: Inclusive Design through Education & Community

description: LG Electronics is creating Home Appliances for All. Through initiatives like LG School and the Bold Move Project, we’ve worked hand-in-hand with diverse customers to build a more inclusive future. This is not just about technology, it’s about listening, learning, and designing together.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identifying insights from LG’s case to apply in inclusive product and service development, and extending them to ESG brand marketing.

Understanding inclusive service design: Developing tailored products and services that reflect the needs of diverse users—including individuals with developmental disabilities, children, and seniors—and leveraging this approach to expand social value and impact.

title: Practical Integration: Learning Accessibility for Blind Students
description: This session offers an authentic, hands-on experience in integrating practical activities that support learning accessibility for blind students, and it brings together teachers, educators, and parents from around the world to exchange ideas.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: To share and exchange idea towards the teaching experience with activities-based learning concept for blind students both in and beyond classroom

title: Using an AI Agent to Assist Student Data Literacy and Exploration
description: Explore datasets through voice or typed commands, and hear data representations described or sonified. An AI-powered agent called DAVAI can facilitate student-directed data inquiry within the context of a free data analysis tool called CODAP.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Show how sonification provides an alternative method to explore data representations.
Illustrate how AI agents can benefit students with mobility and cognitive disabilities, as well as  students who are blind or low-vision.

title: “Shift-up Accessibility” for Effective Accessibility Governance
description: Education leaders and those interested in leadership, please join me to discuss top-down accessibility governance as a strategic leadership imperative. Learn a practical framework that enables your institution to scale accessibility efficiently for supporting equity and achieving compliance.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will gain a greater understanding of how top-down accessibility governance enables organizations to scale accessibility efficiently.
Education leaders will become more aware of how they can manage accessibility as a strategic business imperative.

title: Academic Collaborations for Assistive Technology Devices
description: A collaboration among engineering, occupational therapy, and physical therapy provided custom assistive technology devices for two individuals with disabilities. Students and faculty from across multiple academic departments determined design specifications and produced prototypes.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: To provide design details for a mobile learning station for a child with cerebral palsy, vision, and learning disabilities. To provide design details for an emergency help system for a young woman with speech and mobility disabilities.
To provide a template for how assistive technology device projects can be used by academic engineering departments for real-world design projects for the benefit of students.

title: AI & The Future of Accessible Documents
description: Discover how AI tools support accessible PDF workflows in education, marketing, & technology settings. Learn to integrate automation with expert review to ensure compliance with legal standards while delivering high-quality, user-friendly documents.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Evaluate AI tools for accessible PDF workflows.
Design hybrid AI-human remediation processes.

title: AI as a Workforce Enabler: Empowering Employees with Disabilities
description: Explore how AI language models can empower disabled employees in the workplace by reducing barriers, automating tasks, and enabling greater independence and efficiency.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Evaluate current vs. future readiness of AI tools for workplace accessibility.
Apply a framework for responsibly integrating AI support into employment strategies.

title: Empowering Neurodiverse Users with Text Customization
description: Discover how real-time text customization reduces cognitive load for neurodivergent users. Learn inclusive research methods and gain actionable strategies for deploying empowering, user-centered assistive technologies.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn participatory research methods that involve neurodivergent users in co-creating solutions.
Explore strategies to deploy user-centered assistive tools in classrooms and other real-world settings.

title: How Accessibility is Security

presenters.name: Radostina Tsvetkova
presenters.affiliation: Norwegian Directorate of Labour and Welfare (NAV)

description: Security fails when people can’t use it. This talk links CIA (confidentiality, integrity, availability) with POUR accessibility principles, shows how inaccessible MFA/CAPTCHA create risk, and offers “Secure-by-Inclusion” patterns to cut fraud while ensuring independent, private access.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Spot and fix high-risk patterns_x000D_Identify common “usable-security” traps (e.g., second-device MFA, CAPTCHA, voice/SMS OTP) and replace them with inclusive, attack-resistant alternatives.

Apply Secure-by-Inclusion_x000D_Adopt the Accessible Security Principles to operationalize secure-by-default and secure-by-design practices that work for everyone without disclosing disability or sacrificing independence.

title: Level Up Your Accessibility Procurement Practices

presenters.name: Kelly Melcher
presenters.affiliation: State of Minnesota Department of Human Services

presenters.name: Michelle Engstrom
presenters.affiliation: State of Minnesota Department of Human Services

description: When you're working hard to make sure your organization is practicing digital accessibility, you don't want to forget about procurement. Learn how to ask for accessibility from your vendors and partners, and how to hold them accountable to meet your standards.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Develop methods to learn more about an organization's digital accessibility knowledge, skills, and practice.
Build accountability and follow-up into your purchasing agreements.

title: Making Digital Accessibility Sustainable in the Enterprise
description: It isn’t easy to get accessible. It’s even harder to stay accessible. In this session, we’ll explore how leading enterprises make digital accessibility (a11y) progress last. Join us for an expert discussion on prioritizing a11y early, remediation planning, inclusive hiring, and sustaining success.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: 2. Balance internal teams with external support for optimal results._x000D_Participants will gain an understanding of which accessibility efforts can be outsourced and which require internal expertise for sustainability.

3. Build long-term capabilities and maintain progress as digital experiences evolve. Participants will receive actionable guidance for hiring accessibility experts, dividing responsibilities between teams, and adapting maintenance plans with evolving products.

title: PDF Footnotes & Endnotes: Solutions That Work!
description: Get step-by-step strategies for remediating footnotes, especially inside tables. Improve accessibility, ensure screen readers announce content correctly, and deliver a better user experience.
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how to correct structural issues with nested tags to ensure proper reading by screen readers.
Evaluate options for presenting footnotes inline versus in a dedicated section to improve user experience.

title: Principles of Accessible Documents
description: This session will provide tools and information to allow you to immediately apply the principles of accessibility to your documents. Ensuring equal access to your content for all persons, including persons with disabilities.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Discuss issues commonly missed by automated accessibility checkers and why manual checking is a necessity.
Provide attendees with tools to allow immediate implementation of the information presented in this session.

title: Talk Delivery: Real-Time Contribution for DHH in Online Meetings
description: In online meetings with multiple hearing participants, Deaf and Hard of Hearing (DHH) individuals often face barriers to contributing effectively. This session explores the factors that make participation challenging and introduces methods designed to support more inclusive communication.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Vibe Coding Accessibility
description: Discover how AI coding tools like Copilot and ChatGPT impact accessibility. Explore their strengths, pitfalls, and role in remediation—and see real web and mobile products built with AI that prove accessibility and innovation can vibe together.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply AI to Remediation – Learn how AI can be leveraged to identify and remediate accessibility issues in existing codebases while recognizing potential risks or gaps.
Adopt Best Practices – Gain practical strategies and examples for integrating AI coding tools into development workflows to produce accessible, inclusive digital products.

title: Accessibility from the Start: SAP's Accessibility Design Tools

presenters.name: Irla Bocianoski Rebelo
presenters.role: User Experience Design Expert and Accessibility Lead

description: Explore the benefits of the "shift left" approach for accessibility with SAP's free-to-use Accessibility Design Tools. We will introduce you to this annotation library and plug-in for Figma that can simplify the integration of accessibility aspects early in your design process.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Discover SAP's free accessibility annotations and accessibility plug-in for Figma.
Learn how to use these annotations and enhance the design process.

title: AI as an Accessibility Assistant: A Low-Vision Pipeline for STEM
description: STEM knowledge is born digital yet remains inaccessible to people with low vision. The culprit is PDF. It treats whitespace as invisible. Reflow breaks because words run together. We transform PDFs into a reflow-safe format.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Describe BlankGlyphs and explain how adaptive reclassification preserves boundaries during enlargement.
Trace the page-to-Markdown + LaTeX pipeline and explain how stepwise analysis enables structure-preserving translation.

title: Evaluating Accessible STEM Tools with Single Case Methods
description: Learn to apply single case research methods for evaluating STEM accessibility interventions with students who are blind or have low-vision. Explore how this approach addresses small sample size challenges in low-incidence research.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Compare single case versus group design advantages when samples are small.
Apply visual analysis techniques to interpret individual intervention effects.

title: How Colors & Textures Impact Word Recognition for Neurodivergent
description: Expressive visual design choices can associated with a company’s brand experience, but certain visual elements can also impact individuals in unintended ways. In this session the Oracle Design Research team shares how conducting inclusive UX research can help improve experiences for all users.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: See how the Oracle Design Research team planned and executed research to check a hypothesis about the visual expression of it’s UI and how it might be disproportionately affecting certain users and how the learnings were applied to improve the design for all users.

title: Human Powered  Digital Accessibility in an AI World
description: Discover practical strategies to make digital content accessible through real-world examples and a balanced human plus AI. Learn tools that improve inclusion, reduce risk, and apply across industries to create equitable digital experiences for all.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will learn to identify common barriers to accessibility including cost, complexity, limited awareness, and cultural attitudes. They will gain practical strategies to overcome these challenges, foster inclusive digital environments, and reduce organizational risk.

Attendees will explore seven practical methods to make digital content accessible, from captions and alt text to testing with assistive tech. Real-world examples such as translations, ASL, dubbing, and audio description will show how accessibility improves user experience and inclusion.

title: Inclusive Innovation: Incorporating Privacy into Inclusive Design

presenters.name: Maitreya Shah
presenters.affiliation: American Association of People with Disabilities

presenters.name: Ariana Aboulafia
presenters.role: Project Lead, Disability Rights in Tech Policy

description: AI-enabled assistive technologies benefit disabled people every day – but, sometimes those tools negatively impact personal and data privacy. If you are a developer or start-up entrepreneur who wants a how-to on incorporating privacy into their design process, this sessions for you!
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees should broaden their ability to articulate the business use case of building privacy-protective assistive technologies;

Attendees also should leave with enhanced knowledge of how to incorporate privacy principles, including data minimization and purpose limitation, into the design of assistive technologies; and leave with a desire to speak on these issues or otherwise get involved.

title: Making K-5 Math Accessible: VPAT Remediation at Amplify
description: See how a K–12 educational platform and publisher approaches accessibility remediation in response to external reviews. Four teams share workflows to organize findings and strategies to create accessible art and dynamic alt-text.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate strategies for accessible mathematical content creation – including accessible tech art and dynamic alt text.

title: Mapping to Belonging: Storytelling and Data Tools

presenters.name: Kunal Mehta
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology - University of Washington

presenters.name: Amy Bordenave
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible Technology - University of Washington

presenters.name: Wisam Yasen
presenters.affiliation: Taskar Center for Accessible technology - University of Washington

description: Discover how sidewalk data and OpenSidewalks civic tech tools empower communities to map barriers, tell stories, and advocate for inclusive infrastructure. This session blends accessibility, mapping, and responsible AI to support disability-led data activism.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how storytelling and data visualization intersect
Explore how data can amplify community voice and policy impact and Identify ways to contribute to and use civic data platforms

title: Reading and Writing Math with a Screen Reader: Third Edition

presenters.name: Clint Covington
presenters.role: Principal Program Manager, Experiences + Devices Accessibility

description: Witness math creation using JAWS and NVDA. Over the past year the Microsoft team made major improvements in the interface and documentation of Microsoft Word and PowerPoint. We’ll cover the current state and look at what is coming.
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Attendees will learn how the MathCAT enabling technology is making math accessible to screen reader users on many platforms.
Attendees will learn about MathML that is being used for the provision of educational course content on the web and in digital textbooks.

title: Solo to Strategic: Building Enterprise A11y from Ground Up
description: Learn battle-tested strategies for building enterprise accessibility programs as a solo practitioner. Discover frameworks for stakeholder buy-in, cross-functional collaboration, and creating scalable tools that transform organizational culture without breaking budgets.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Build collaborative frameworks with Legal, Risk, Marketing, Education, Belonging, Clinicial, Product, and Engineering teams to embed accessibility into existing workflows
Create scalable tools and processes including annotation kits, design system integration, and enterprise working groups for sustainability

title: The Sight Loss Community is Conflicted about AI
description: Based on an extensive Canadian survey, this session shows what persons with sight loss need to trust AI: clear benefits, human-in-the-loop safeguards, and accessible understanding.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a

title: Voice ATs in the Age of AI
description: Windows voice access offers free, built-in dictation and hands-free control, empowering users with mobility needs. This session covers its newer AI-powered features, practical tips for leveraging voice and a look ahead at how voice will integrate with AI assistants to further broaden its usability.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Learn how they can move to use free voice access tool over Dragon and gain deeper insights into design & technical improvements
Learn practical strategies for using Voice access in real-world scenarios in the current ecosystem with AI

title: Accessibility Architecture at Scale: A Strategic Playbook
description: Learn a proven framework for scaling accessibility across multiple brands and platforms with limited resources. This strategic playbook covers governance, prioritization, and integration into engineering workflows.
audience_level: Advanced: Considerable experience with topic; strategy and expert level discussion of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Implement prioritization and triage strategies to maximize impact with limited resources.
Embed accessibility checkpoints into engineering and QA processes without slowing delivery.

title: AI for Touch: Generative Tools for Scalable Tactile Access
description: This session explores how generative AI tools are transforming tactile learning. Learn how platforms like Dot Vista use AI to create tactile-ready graphics on demand, expanding access while supporting best practices in tactile design.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify appropriate use cases for AI-generated vs. professionally designed tactile graphics.
Consider responsible implementation strategies that preserve educational quality and user agency.

title: ARIA for Non-Coders: What Designers Really Need to Know
description: Learn what ARIA really means for designers without coding skills. This session simplifies ARIA into practical concepts, showing when it matters, when it doesn’t, and how to design accessibly while partnering effectively with developers.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: Identify common interactive design patterns (such as modals, tabs, accordions, and menus) where ARIA is most relevant and how to account for them during the design phase.
Learn how to avoid common ARIA misuse by prioritizing semantic HTML in design specs and gain strategies to communicate accessibility needs effectively to developers.

title: CANCELLED: FCC Disability Rights Office: What You Need to Know
description: Come find out what the FCC’s Disability Rights Office does, and how it affects your day-to-day life. A DRO attorney will provide an update on several hot-button FCC proceedings, unravel the mysteries of closed captioning regulations, and take questions from the audience.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Answer common questions about the FCC’s jurisdiction over different kinds of media
Inform attendees about the current accessibility obligations covered entities should be complying with

title: Implementing AI Supports for Low Vision Tasks
description: See practical, entertaining demos of how AI augments magnification and reading for people with low vision across live camera, distance, files, and books; plus clear guardrails for privacy and accuracy, and a simple checklist to choose AI vs. OCR vs. hybrid workflows.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply a simple decision checklist to choose AI, OCR, or hybrid workflows for reading, distance viewing, and saved documents.
Configure privacy/safety controls (consent prompts, feature lock, usage limits) for institutional settings.

title: Mandate to Momentum: Partnering to Build an Accessibility Program
description: One district, with a consultant, turned an OCR complaint into an executed digital accessibility plan. Gain strategies for vendor engagement, training, compliance, and sustainable practices across education, government, and tech.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Apply strategies for engaging stakeholders and vendors in accessibility compliance.
Recognize the legal and policy landscape shaping accessibility requirements._x000D_Adapt lessons from the case study to their own institutional or organizational context.

title: Not a Fight, but a Tag Team: Building the Accessibility Future

presenters.name: Bill Curtis-Davidson
presenters.affiliation: Georgia Tech Center for Inclusive Design and Innovation (CIDI)

presenters.name: Samantha Evans
presenters.affiliation: International Association of Accessibility Professionals (IAAP), A Division of G3ict

description: Five accessibility leaders enter a metaphorical boxing ring, not to fight each other, but to tag-team against exclusion. Each plays a unique role; together they show how collaboration beats inaccessible design. Accessibility wins when we unite.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Use a boxing metaphor to highlight collaboration over competition in accessibility.
Share real-world strategies to fight exclusion as a united team.

title: Tendrils & Teeth: The Difficult Work of Accessibility Policies
description: It should be the foremost concern of those writing a Digital Accessibility Policy how likely it will be the Policy can affect change. In this session, we’ll detail the difficult, looping work of writing a Policy, and how it involves a firm insistence that the Policy have tendrils and teeth.
audience_level: Intermediate: Basic to mid-level knowledge of topic; sharing of smart practices and real-world application of the subject matter.
learning_objectives: Understand how Digital Accessibility Policies can become ineffective, or quickly undone.
Understand a series of tools and methodologies for writing and publishing a more effective Digital Accessibility Policy.

title: Voicing Response System for Inclusive Online STEM Simulations

presenters.name: Taliesin Smith
presenters.affiliation: PhET Interactive Simulations, University of Colorado

presenters.name: Jesse Greenberg
presenters.affiliation: PhET Interactive Simulations, University of Colorado Boulder

description: The Voicing Response System (VRS) is a web-based auditory description framework in PhET simulations that expands access to STEM learning. Built on SceneryStack and using the Description Design Framework, it delivers customizable, context-aware descriptions through the browser to complement screen readers.
audience_level: Beginning: Minimal knowledge of topic; how-to instruction of a new subject matter.
learning_objectives: n/a
